response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and web engineer with breadth across TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this embedded networking security role. He lacks the required native-level Japanese proficiency entirely (English, Romanian, basic German only), shows no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and has only brief C/C++ exposure (roughly seven months at National Instruments on IDE tooling, not embedded firmware). There is no evidence of Layer-2/Layer-3 networking, MACsec, 802.1X, or any of the security technologies central to the position.
strengths: Long, continuous software-development career well beyond the 3-year minimum
Some systems-level exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux familiarity
Strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling) and open-source contributions
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a hard requirement for this role
No Linux kernel analysis/modification, embedded firmware, or networking-security (MACsec, 802.1X) experience
Career trajectory is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React), far from C-based UNIX embedded development
